moguls.name: Elon Musk
moguls.title: CEO, Tesla & SpaceX; founder, xAI
moguls.knownFor: World's first trillionaire after the SpaceX IPO; rockets, robotaxis, and posting
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Tesla FY2024 proxy ($57,243)

council.quip: Caught a rocket with chopsticks and a trillion with memes. GAMING — but somebody take the phone away at 3am.
Booked Mars, shipped in public, and never skipped a dramatic tweet. Big ideas, big noise, real hustle.

moguls.name: Larry Page
moguls.title: Co-founder, Google (Alphabet)
moguls.knownFor: Co-invented the search engine that answers everything, including 'who is Larry Page'
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Alphabet FY2025 proxy ($310,826)

council.quip: Invented the machine that answers everything, then vanished so hard it can't find him. Respect the exit strategy.
From a dorm room to the world’s search bar, he built the shortcut for everything and still talks softly.

moguls.name: Sergey Brin
moguls.title: Co-founder, Google (Alphabet)
moguls.knownFor: Google's other co-founder; back in the building helping steer Alphabet's AI push
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Alphabet FY2025 proxy ($310,826)

council.quip: Back from retirement for the AI arc like a reunion tour. Cool cameo, but the encore is still buffering.
Co-built Google and still gets to steer AI from the back row. Quiet, rich, and dangerous when caffeinated.

moguls.name: Jeff Bezos
moguls.title: Founder & Executive Chairman, Amazon; co-CEO, Project Prometheus
moguls.knownFor: Built the everything store, then left to do rockets and a new AI startup
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Amazon FY2024 proxy ($37,181)

council.quip: Built the everything store, then left to collect rockets and startups. The bald head is pure aerodynamic ambition.
Built the everything store, then bounced to rockets and AI startups. Legendary hustle, with a very extra side quest.

moguls.name: Michael Dell
moguls.title: Chairman & CEO, Dell Technologies
moguls.knownFor: Started selling PCs from a dorm room; the AI-server boom made 'Dude, you're getting a Dell' a $235B punchline
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Dell FY2026 proxy ($78,796)

council.quip: Dorm room to $235B because AI needs somewhere to live. Dude, you're getting a Dell — forever, apparently.
Started in a dorm with used PCs; now your AI stack probably has a Dell in it. Proper DIY-to-colossus energy.

moguls.name: Mark Zuckerberg
moguls.title: Co-founder & CEO, Meta
moguls.knownFor: Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and a famously symbolic $1 salary
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Meta FY2025 proxy ($388,200)

council.quip: The $1 salary next to $194B is the most expensive PR stunt alive. The metaverse legs saw everything. FRAUD!
One-dollar salary, billion-dollar network, and a social feed stretching back to your awkward teenage profile.

moguls.name: Larry Ellison
moguls.title: Co-founder, Chairman & CTO, Oracle
moguls.knownFor: Database king turned AI-cloud and media dealmaker; owns most of a Hawaiian island
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Oracle FY2025 proxy (188:1 ratio, ~$159,574)

council.quip: Owns most of an island, but the real estate he truly loves is your license renewal. Eternal audit energy.
Database king turned island mogul; big brain, big boats, and maybe bigger closets than necessary.

moguls.name: Jensen Huang
moguls.title: Co-founder & CEO, Nvidia
moguls.knownFor: The leather-jacketed chip chief whose GPUs power the entire AI boom
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Nvidia FY2025 proxy ($301,233)

council.quip: One leather jacket powering the whole AI economy. Sells shovels in a gold rush he personally started. GAMING.
Leather jacket legend. If an AI startup can run, thank Nvidia chips and one relentless man in a jacket.

moguls.name: Bernard Arnault
moguls.title: Chairman & CEO, LVMH
moguls.knownFor: Europe's luxury emperor: Louis Vuitton, Dior, and roughly 75 brands you can't afford
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: n/a (LVMH foreign issuer, no SEC pay-ratio disclosure)

council.quip: Sells the same canvas bag through 75 brands at 75 prices. Luxury is a vibe tax and he is the collector.
Luxury CEO of 75 aspirational brands. Real business muscle, but with more logos than sleep.

moguls.name: Warren Buffett
moguls.title: Chairman, Berkshire Hathaway
moguls.knownFor: The Oracle of Omaha; handed the CEO keys to Greg Abel but kept the chairman's chair and the Cherry Coke
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-01 | median: Berkshire FY2024 proxy ($82,106)

council.quip: Handed Greg the keys, kept the Cherry Coke and the aura. Compound interest in a cardigan. GAMING.
Oracle of Omaha energy: compound value, keep it simple, and still keep the Cherry Coke throne.

moguls.name: Amancio Ortega
moguls.title: Founder, Inditex (Zara)
moguls.knownFor: Reclusive Spanish fast-fashion pioneer who turned Zara into a retail empire
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-03 | median: n/a (Inditex foreign issuer, no SEC pay-ratio disclosure)

council.quip: $144B on clothes engineered to survive exactly five washes. Efficient? Yes. Vibes? Machine-washable.
Built Zara by making style move faster than everyone else. Efficient, ruthless, and very anti-casual.

moguls.name: Rob Walton
moguls.title: Walmart heir; former Chairman, Walmart
moguls.knownFor: Eldest Walmart heir; also bought the Denver Broncos, because why not
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-02 | median: Walmart FY2025 proxy ($29,469)

council.quip: Speedran a $132B fortune by being born, then impulse-bought the Broncos. Inheritance is not a grindset. FRAUD!
Inherited a grocery dynasty and bought a football team too. Solid legacy energy, not exactly garage grind.

moguls.name: Jim Walton
moguls.title: Walmart heir; Arvest Bank
moguls.knownFor: The banking Walton; runs the family's Arvest Bank empire in Arkansas
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-02 | median: Walmart FY2025 proxy ($29,469)

council.quip: The Walton who actually clocks in — running a bank in Arkansas is the most modest $129B flex imaginable.
Family wealth, banking branch, and Arkansas-level calm. Not a startup, but not a joke either.

moguls.name: Steve Ballmer
moguls.title: Former CEO, Microsoft; owner, LA Clippers
moguls.knownFor: Yelled 'developers!' at Microsoft, now yells courtside at his own NBA team
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-03 | median: Microsoft FY2025 proxy ($200,972)

council.quip: Screamed DEVELOPERS into $126B and now cheers courtside like he won the Clippers in a raffle. Joy is GAMING.
Yelled 'developers!' into the void, now yells courtside. Built a giant, bought a bigger one, still loud.

moguls.name: Carlos Slim Helú
moguls.title: Honorary Chairman, América Móvil; Grupo Carso
moguls.knownFor: Mexico's telecom titan; was the world's richest person before it was cool
moguls.source: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires, 2026-07-02 | median: n/a (America Movil foreign private issuer, no SEC pay-ratio disclosure)

council.quip: Was the world's richest off phone bills before it was cool. Now 'honorary' everything — legacy mode activated.
Telecom czar before it was cool: bought connectivity at national scale and kept it profitable.

moguls.name: Niraj Shah
moguls.title: Co-founder & CEO, Wayfair
moguls.knownFor: 2025's highest-paid CEO — a $280.8M package (mostly a one-time multi-year stock grant) for selling you couches
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23); net worth Forbes 2026 est. | median: Wayfair FY2025 proxy ($49,254; 5,702:1 ratio)

council.quip: $280M in one year for selling couches — the sectional isn't the only thing overstuffed. FRAUD!
A $280.8M couch royalty. Nice furniture, but this paystub reads like an upscale tax on your dining room.

moguls.name: Hock Tan
moguls.title: President & CEO, Broadcom
moguls.knownFor: Semiconductor dealmaker-in-chief; serial acquirer whose chips quietly run the internet
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: Broadcom FY2025 proxy ($378,281; 543:1 ratio)

council.quip: Collects companies like infinity stones and pays himself like the snap. $205M of pure acquisition aura.
Chip whisperer and deal avalanche creator. If the internet is connected, he likely got a memo and a bonus.

moguls.name: Peter Gassner
moguls.title: Founder & CEO, Veeva Systems
moguls.knownFor: Built the cloud software big pharma runs on; the biggest paycheck you've never heard of
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23); net worth Forbes real-time est. 2026 | median: Veeva FY2025 proxy (1,250:1 ratio, ~$137,949)

council.quip: Built the boring software pharma can't quit and quietly banked $172M. Anonymous kings stay GAMING.
Built software for pharma factories and gets a paycheck so normal it barely fits a headline.

moguls.name: David Solomon
moguls.title: Chairman & CEO, Goldman Sachs
moguls.knownFor: Wall Street's top banker and part-time DJ (D-Sol), now with a nine-figure paycheck
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: Goldman Sachs FY2025 proxy ($160,667; ~740:1 ratio)

council.quip: Nine figures a year AND a DJ alias. D-Sol's biggest drop is the fee schedule. Read the room, then invoice it.
Part-time DJ, full-time banker; if the playlist improves, so does the payout. Wall Street meets nightclub.

moguls.name: Sridhar Ramaswamy
moguls.title: CEO, Snowflake
moguls.knownFor: Ex-Google ads boss now paid $100M+ to run the data cloud everyone's AI drinks from
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: Snowflake FY2026 proxy (~$287,200)

council.quip: $100M to keep the data cloud hydrated for everyone else's AI. Solid gig, but he's the water boy, not the game.
Runs a data kingdom everyone drinks from and takes a nine-figure tab for it. Respect with a high invoice.

moguls.name: Nikesh Arora
moguls.title: Chairman & CEO, Palo Alto Networks
moguls.knownFor: Cybersecurity's best-paid bodyguard; perennial fixture on highest-paid CEO lists
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: Palo Alto Networks FY2025 proxy (442:1 ratio, ~$225,648)

council.quip: Cybersecurity's priciest bodyguard: $99.7M a year to stand by the door. The comp committee needs a firewall.
Cybersecurity bodyguard for the corporate world, paid like he personally wrestled every breach solo.

moguls.name: Satya Nadella
moguls.title: Chairman & CEO, Microsoft
moguls.knownFor: Turned Microsoft into a $3T+ AI juggernaut; owns just 0.01% of it and is a billionaire anyway
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23); net worth Forbes 2026 est. | median: Microsoft FY2025 proxy ($200,972; 480:1 ratio)

council.quip: Owns 0.01% of Microsoft, made it worth $3T, billionaire anyway. Minority stake, majority aura. GAMING.
Turned a legacy titan into an AI juggernaut. Built real execution and still keeps low-key billionaire vibes.

moguls.name: Charles Scharf
moguls.title: CEO, Wells Fargo
moguls.knownFor: Got Wells Fargo out of the Fed's penalty box, then got paid like it
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: Wells Fargo FY2025 proxy ($82,044; 1,152:1 ratio)

council.quip: Got Wells Fargo out of the penalty box, then billed like a playoff lawyer. Fair work, but $94M is a victory lap.
Steered a scandal-warmed bank back to center and then collected a 'thank you' big enough to notice.

moguls.name: Lip-Bu Tan
moguls.title: CEO, Intel
moguls.knownFor: Chip-industry legend hired to rescue Intel; a billionaire before he even took the job
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23); net worth Bloomberg Billionaires Index | median: Intel FY2025 proxy ($114,900; 812:1 ratio)

council.quip: $93M to rescue Intel, billionaire before day one. Verdict pending until the chips actually ship. Aight, for now.
Came to Intel as rescue crew with billionaire backup and a 100-dollar bill-sized salary demand.

moguls.name: Robin Vince
moguls.title: President & CEO, BNY (Bank of New York Mellon)
moguls.knownFor: Runs America's oldest bank (founded 1784); Alexander Hamilton's startup now pays $83M a year
moguls.source: Equilar 100 (FY2025, publ. 2026-04-23) | median: BNY FY2025 proxy ($81,987; ~1,018:1 ratio)

council.quip: $83M to run history's fanciest coat check — Hamilton's bank just holds other people's stuff. Custody vibes!
Runs one of America’s oldest banks and gets Hamilton-grade pay for keeping the old books alive.
